Telnyx AI
Primary agent entrypoint: https://telnyx.com/agents/start

Summary
Telnyx is a communications and AI infrastructure platform for agents that need programmable voice, AI assistants, messaging, phone numbers, SIP trunking, inference, webhook delivery, and MCP access. This file is the text-first retrieval index for the stable agent-consumption artifacts that should be visible without JavaScript.

Named discovery targets that should resolve directly from this file include Telnyx Auth, Telnyx MCP, Telnyx OpenAPI, Telnyx Agent Skills, Telnyx Webhooks, Telnyx Voice AI Agents, and Telnyx x402 Payments.

Getting started
- Zero-signup evaluation path: read https://telnyx.com/auth.md, inspect https://telnyx.com/.well-known/agent-access.json, then call POST https://telnyx.com/api/inference
- Account-creation path: read https://telnyx.com/agent-signup.md, complete the bot-signup plus email-link flow, then use Authorization: Bearer <TELNYX_API_KEY>
- First authenticated API check: call https://api.telnyx.com/v2/available_phone_numbers with the bearer key, or initialize https://api.telnyx.com/v2/mcp
- First Voice AI onboarding path: start with https://telnyx.com/guides/ai-assistants.md and continue with https://telnyx.com/guides/voice-agent-onboarding.md
- CLI bootstrap after key creation: run telnyx-agent status --json

What an agent should learn first
- Telnyx exposes both direct REST discovery and MCP discovery.
- The exact first-run evaluation path is POST https://telnyx.com/api/inference, which is no-auth for the caller but host-authenticated by Telnyx.
- Governed execution metadata includes risk_class, approval_expectation, approval_path, memory_scope, model_behavior, and audit_identifiers so agents can preserve reviewable correlation IDs.
- The stable auth contract lives in the public auth and protected-resource documents.
- The named assistant guides are the fastest path for voice-assistant discovery, and the hosted OpenAI examples there currently use openai/gpt-5.4.
- Telnyx Webhooks has an explicit public guide for webhook setup, signature verification, delivery inspection, and retry behavior.
- Telnyx Voice AI Agents has explicit crawlable guides for assistant creation and voice-agent onboarding.
- Telnyx x402 Payments has an explicit crawlable guide for quote creation, EIP-712 signing, and account funding on Base.
- Governed examples exist for agents that should not begin with broad raw-tool access.

Core discovery surfaces
- Agent manifest: https://telnyx.com/.well-known/agent-card.json
- Agent access: https://telnyx.com/.well-known/agent-access.json
- Agent skills index: https://telnyx.com/.well-known/agent-skills/index.json
- Auth guide: https://telnyx.com/auth.md
- OAuth authorization server: https://api.telnyx.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
- OAuth protected resource: https://api.telnyx.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
- MCP resource metadata: https://api.telnyx.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/v2/mcp
- MCP server card: https://telnyx.com/.well-known/mcp/server-card.json
- MCP endpoint: https://api.telnyx.com/v2/mcp
- MCP Apps registry: https://developers.telnyx.com/.well-known/mcp-app-registry.json
- MCP Apps registry alias: https://developers.telnyx.com/.well-known/mcp-apps.json
- MCP Apps catalog: https://developers.telnyx.com/apps
- MCP Apps proof app: https://developers.telnyx.com/apps/number-intelligence
- OpenAPI: https://telnyx.com/.well-known/openapi.json
- Capabilities: https://telnyx.com/ai/capabilities.json
- Pricing: https://telnyx.com/ai/pricing.json
- Telnyx Webhooks guide: https://developers.telnyx.com/development/api-fundamentals/webhooks/receiving-webhooks
- Telnyx Webhooks repo mirror: https://telnyx.com/guides/webhooks.md
- Telnyx Voice AI Agents guide: https://telnyx.com/guides/ai-assistants.md
- Voice-agent onboarding guide: https://telnyx.com/guides/voice-agent-onboarding.md
- Telnyx x402 Payments guide: https://telnyx.com/guides/x402-payments.md
- Signup guide: https://telnyx.com/agent-signup.md

Source of truth
- Repo-owned source mirrors: https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/agents/start.md, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/auth.md, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/agent-signup.md, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/llms.txt, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/.well-known/agent-access.json, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/.well-known/agent-card.json, https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/ai/capabilities.json, and https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/blob/main/ai/pricing.json
- Docs-hosted live webhooks guide: https://developers.telnyx.com/development/api-fundamentals/webhooks/receiving-webhooks
- Docs-hosted live MCP Apps discovery: https://developers.telnyx.com/apps and https://developers.telnyx.com/.well-known/mcp-app-registry.json

How to choose a surface
- Use POST https://telnyx.com/api/inference when the goal is first-run evaluation without creating an account first.
- Use https://api.telnyx.com/v2/mcp when the agent already speaks MCP and should discover tools dynamically.
- Use https://telnyx.com/.well-known/openapi.json when the workflow needs endpoint-level REST exploration or code generation.
- Use https://developers.telnyx.com/apps when the workflow should stay on a focused, governed MCP App with narrower tool exposure.
- Use https://telnyx.com/guides/ai-assistants.md and https://telnyx.com/guides/voice-agent-onboarding.md when the task is specifically first-run assistant creation, answer-webhook wiring, or GPT-5.4-hosted assistant evaluation.
- Use https://telnyx.com/guides/x402-payments.md when the task is specifically x402 account funding.
- Read the Telnyx Webhooks guide at https://developers.telnyx.com/development/api-fundamentals/webhooks/receiving-webhooks before implementing inbound webhook handlers or retry logic.

Governed examples
- OpenAI, LangChain, and CrewAI governed examples: https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/tree/main/tools/python/examples
- Runtime instructions: https://telnyx.com/AGENTS.md
- Auth source: https://telnyx.com/auth.md
- This file: https://telnyx.com/llms.txt
- Local manifest mirror: https://telnyx.com/agent.json

Recommended path
1. Start at https://telnyx.com/agents/start
2. Read https://telnyx.com/auth.md
3. Use https://telnyx.com/.well-known/agent-access.json for the demo-first path and current signup contract
4. Decide between POST https://telnyx.com/api/inference for a zero-signup first call or https://telnyx.com/agent-signup.md for authenticated account creation
5. Use https://api.telnyx.com/v2/mcp for MCP or https://telnyx.com/.well-known/openapi.json for REST discovery after auth is in place
6. Read the Telnyx Webhooks guide at https://developers.telnyx.com/development/api-fundamentals/webhooks/receiving-webhooks before implementing inbound webhook handlers
7. Review https://github.com/team-telnyx/ai/tree/main/tools/python/examples when you need governed examples instead of broad raw-tool access
